statement.normalized: Let $p(n)$ denote the partition function of $n$ and let $F(n)$ count the number of distinct prime factors of\[\prod_{1\leq k\leq n}p(k).\]Does $F(n)\to \infty$ with $n$? Is $F(n)>n$ for all sufficiently large $n$?
statement.original: Let $p(n)$ denote the partition function of $n$ and let $F(n)$ count the number of distinct prime factors of\[\prod_{1\leq k\leq n}p(k).\]Does $F(n)\to \infty$ with $n$? Is $F(n)>n$ for all sufficiently large $n$?
